-- ── RLS: chat.message_attachments UPDATE ────────────────────
-- No UPDATE policy existed on this table.
-- The sender of the message may write back the media_asset_id for their own
-- attachments. Uses the actor_owners ownership model (same pattern as the
-- 20260430200000_fix_chat_rls_multi_actor migration).

CREATE POLICY "attachment sender can write back media_asset_id"
  ON chat.message_attachments
  FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM chat.messages m
      JOIN vc.actor_owners ao ON ao.actor_id = m.sender_actor_id
      WHERE m.id   = message_attachments.message_id
        AND ao.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND COALESCE(ao.is_void, false) = false
    )
  )
  WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM chat.messages m
      JOIN vc.actor_owners ao ON ao.actor_id = m.sender_actor_id
      WHERE m.id   = message_attachments.message_id
        AND ao.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND COALESCE(ao.is_void, false) = false
    )
  );

-- ── RLS: chat.message_attachments UPDATE ────────────────────
-- No UPDATE policy existed on this table.
-- The sender of the message may write back the media_asset_id for their own
-- attachments. Uses the actor_owners ownership model (same pattern as the
-- 20260430200000_fix_chat_rls_multi_actor migration).

CREATE POLICY "attachment sender can write back media_asset_id"
  ON chat.message_attachments
  FOR UPDATE
  TO authenticated
  USING (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM chat.messages m
      JOIN vc.actor_owners ao ON ao.actor_id = m.sender_actor_id
      WHERE m.id   = message_attachments.message_id
        AND ao.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND COALESCE(ao.is_void, false) = false
    )
  )
  WITH CHECK (
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM chat.messages m
      JOIN vc.actor_owners ao ON ao.actor_id = m.sender_actor_id
      WHERE m.id   = message_attachments.message_id
        AND ao.user_id = auth.uid()
        AND COALESCE(ao.is_void, false) = false
    )
  );

GRANT EXECUTE housekeeping (see bottom).
--
-- NOTE: "Private actor" in this system means hidden from anonymous search only.
-- All authenticated users who own at least one actor see private actors. This
-- is the current platform design — no relationship/follower gate is added here.
-- If the intent is follower-only discoverability, a separate migration is
-- required. This migration only fixes the ownership spoofing gap.
--
-- DEPLOYMENT: Never run automatically. Owner deploys manually via Supabase SQL
-- editor after reviewing this file in full.
-- =============================================================================

create or replace function identity.search_actor_directory(
  p_viewer_domain    text,
  p_viewer_actor_id  uuid,
  p_query            text    default null::text,
  p_filter           text    default 'all'::text,
  p_limit            integer default 20,
  p_offset           integer default 0
)
returns table(
  result_type          text,
  actor_domain         text,
  actor_id             uuid,
  actor_kind           text,
  display_name         text,
  username             text,
  avatar_url           text,
  banner_url           text,
  bio                  text,
  is_private           boolean,
  is_listable_in_app   boolean,
  discoverable         boolean,
  publish              boolean,
  rank                 double precision
)
language sql
stable
as $function$
with

-- ─── FIX A: verify caller owns p_viewer_actor_id before trusting it ───────────
--
-- If p_viewer_actor_id is supplied but auth.uid() does not own it (via
-- vc.actor_owners), we treat the viewer as null (anonymous / unauthenticated).
-- This closes the cross-actor spoofing and block-bypass gaps.
--
-- Why downgrade rather than throw?
-- Throwing would break unauthenticated SSR/Edge calls that supply null, and
-- would also surface actor ownership information to the caller through error
-- messages. Silent downgrade is safe and preserves the public-only path.
--
verified_viewer as (
  select
    case
      when p_viewer_actor_id is null then null::uuid
      when auth.uid() is null        then null::uuid
      when exists (
        select 1
        from   vc.actor_owners ao
        where  ao.actor_id = p_viewer_actor_id
          and  ao.user_id  = auth.uid()
      ) then p_viewer_actor_id
      else null::uuid                               -- forged/unowned → downgrade
    end as viewer_actor_id
),

normalized_input as (
  select
    nullif(btrim(coalesce(p_query, '')), '') as q,
    lower(nullif(btrim(coalesce(p_query, '')), '')) as q_lower,
    -- FIX B: server-side cap on p_limit (max 100 per page)
    least(greatest(coalesce(p_limit, 20), 1), 100) as lim,
    greatest(coalesce(p_offset, 0), 0) as off,
    -- Use verified viewer, not the raw parameter
    (select viewer_actor_id from verified_viewer) as viewer_id,
    case
      when (select viewer_actor_id from verified_viewer) is null then 'public'
      when p_filter in ('all', 'users', 'vports') then p_filter
      else 'all'
    end as filter_key
),

candidate_rows as (
  select
    ad.actor_domain,
    ad.actor_id,
    ad.actor_kind,
    ad.display_name,
    ad.username,
    ad.avatar_url,
    ad.banner_url,
    ad.bio,
    ad.is_private,
    ad.is_listable_in_app,
    ad.discoverable,
    ad.publish,
    ni.q,
    ni.q_lower,
    ni.viewer_id,

    case
      when ni.q is null then 0::double precision
      when lower(coalesce(ad.username, '')) = ni.q_lower then 1000::double precision
      when lower(coalesce(ad.display_name, '')) = ni.q_lower then 950::double precision
      when lower(coalesce(ad.username, '')) like ni.q_lower || '%' then
        800::double precision + similarity(lower(coalesce(ad.username, '')), ni.q_lower)
      when lower(coalesce(ad.display_name, '')) like ni.q_lower || '%' then
        700::double precision + similarity(lower(coalesce(ad.display_name, '')), ni.q_lower)
      else greatest(
        similarity(lower(coalesce(ad.username, '')), ni.q_lower),
        similarity(lower(coalesce(ad.display_name, '')), ni.q_lower)
      )
    end as rank_score
  from identity.actor_directory ad
  cross join normalized_input ni
  where
    ad.is_active = true
    and ad.is_void = false
    and ad.is_hydratable = true
    and ad.is_listable_in_app = true
    and ad.discoverable = true
    and ad.publish = true

    -- DB safety invariant (unchanged):
    -- anonymous/null viewer must never receive private actors.
    -- Now "viewer" is the VERIFIED viewer, not the raw parameter.
    and (
      ni.viewer_id is not null
      or ad.is_private = false
    )

    and (
      ni.filter_key = 'all'
      or ni.filter_key = 'public'
      or (ni.filter_key = 'users'  and ad.actor_kind = 'user')
      or (ni.filter_key = 'vports' and ad.actor_kind = 'vport')
    )

    and (
      ni.q is null
      or lower(coalesce(ad.username, '')) like '%' || ni.q_lower || '%'
      or lower(coalesce(ad.display_name, '')) like '%' || ni.q_lower || '%'
      or lower(coalesce(ad.username, '')) % ni.q_lower
      or lower(coalesce(ad.display_name, '')) % ni.q_lower
    )
),

-- FIX A (block check): use verified viewer_id, not raw p_viewer_actor_id.
-- With the original code, a caller passing actor_Y's UUID would have their
-- block check run against actor_Y's blocks, not their own. This is now fixed —
-- the block check uses the ownership-verified viewer_id.
unblocked as (
  select c.*
  from candidate_rows c
  where not exists (
    select 1
    from moderation.blocks b
    where b.status = 'active'
      and (
        (
          b.blocker_domain   = p_viewer_domain
          and b.blocker_actor_id = c.viewer_id    -- was: p_viewer_actor_id
          and b.blocked_domain   = c.actor_domain
          and b.blocked_actor_id = c.actor_id
        )
        or
        (
          b.blocker_domain   = c.actor_domain
          and b.blocker_actor_id = c.actor_id
          and b.blocked_domain   = p_viewer_domain
          and b.blocked_actor_id = c.viewer_id    -- was: p_viewer_actor_id
        )
      )
  )
)

select
  'actor'::text as result_type,
  u.actor_domain,
  u.actor_id,
  u.actor_kind,
  u.display_name,
  u.username,
  u.avatar_url,
  u.banner_url,
  u.bio,
  u.is_private,
  u.is_listable_in_app,
  u.discoverable,
  u.publish,
  u.rank_score as rank
from unblocked u
cross join normalized_input ni
where
  ni.q is null
  or u.rank_score > 0.05
order by
  u.rank_score desc,
  lower(coalesce(u.display_name, '')) asc,
  lower(coalesce(u.username, '')) asc,
  u.actor_id asc
limit (select lim from normalized_input)
offset (select off from normalized_input);

GRANT HOUSEKEEPING
--
-- Ensure anon role does NOT have EXECUTE on this function.
-- (IDRLS-001: anon execute on identity.refresh_actor_directory_row was already
-- revoked. This migration extends that hygiene to search_actor_directory.)
